summary: One Jest test failed: a string equality assertion comparing a registry URL without a trailing slash to the command output which contained a trailing slash.
root_cause: The test 'pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc' expected the registry value 'https://my-org.registry.example.com' but the command output included a trailing slash 'https://my-org.registry.example.com/', causing an exact-match assertion to fail.
relevant_tests: pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc

evidence.quote: ● pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc
evidence.reason: Identifies the specific failing test case.
The value the test expected (without trailing slash).
The actual command output contained a trailing slash, causing the mismatch.
Shows the exact assertion in the test that compares the command output to the expected string.

suggested_fix: Either normalize the registry URL before printing (remove any trailing slash) so 'pnpm config get' outputs 'https://my-org.registry.example.com', or relax the test to compare normalized URLs (e.g. trim a trailing slash before comparing or use a URL-aware comparison).
